preamble: The shift from [d] to [tɬ] is a rare process involving affrication and lateralization, typically seen in specialized dialectal developments.

examples.note: A complex shift involving lateral release and loss of voicing.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
